user_feedback: now, instead of the stick being perpendicular to the plane of the floor, I want it to be parallel so it looks like the player is holding the sword, and also we don't want the monster to move the player, when the monster should stop moving when the player is in range of the attack
analysis:
- Issue identified: 
  1. The weapon (sword) is currently positioned vertically (perpendicular to floor) instead of horizontally (parallel to floor) as if being held
  2. The monster continues moving when close to player and also pushes the player during collision, when it should stop at attack range and only the monster should be pushed back

- Files affected: 
  - entities.js (weapon rotation/position, monster chase behavior)
  - physics.js (collision handling to prevent monster from pushing player)

- Changes needed:
  1. Rotate the weapon 90 degrees to make it horizontal/parallel to floor
  2. Adjust weapon position to look like it's being held by the player
  3. Modify monster's chasePlayer() method to stop moving when within attack range
  4. Update collision handling to only push monster back, not the player
